tailwinds.name: Physical World AI
tailwinds.why: The loudest 2026 signal: AI moving from words to the physical world. Seedcamp just built a $320M fund around it; defense, robotics and space are drawing early capital. Vision-model quality crossed "genuinely good" as inference cost fell ~10x.
tailwinds.watch: Multi-customer industrial/defense deployments proving the economics before national scale — the constrained-geography proof point.

tailwinds.name: Agent-native infrastructure & AgentOps
tailwinds.why: a16z: the enterprise backend isn’t built for one agent goal to fan out into 5,000 sub-tasks in milliseconds. The control plane is being re-architected for agent-speed workloads — and the AgentOps orchestration layer is where the moat lives.
tailwinds.watch: Bottom-up developer adoption with zero paid acquisition (GitHub stars, Show HN) — the GitHub/Stripe pattern.

tailwinds.name: Unstructured-data cleanup & RL/eval infra
tailwinds.why: a16z’s "data entropy": 80% of corporate knowledge is unstructured and decaying. Enterprises need continuous clean/structure/validate/govern so downstream AI works. Frontier labs are shifting from scraped to curated data + RL/eval — infra appreciates 2–3 yrs before apps (Gil).
tailwinds.watch: Adoption by frontier labs / large enterprises as the reliability layer — the tell before it’s obvious.

tailwinds.name: AI-native services & digital labor
tailwinds.why: Sequoia: the next wave is AI-native services that sell completed work, not seats. PitchBook calls it a margin super-cycle from seat-based licenses to outcome-based digital labor. GTM, delivery and workforce ops are being rebuilt agent-first.
tailwinds.watch: Outcome/usage pricing + becoming the system of record vs. a feature; net retention >120%.

tailwinds.name: Autonomous back-office finance & GRC
tailwinds.why: AP, tax, collections and compliance software has been structurally unchanged for a decade; AI agents now do the work end-to-end. Finance teams already improvise with LLMs — the behavior is underway, the reliable infrastructure is the opening.
tailwinds.watch: Ownership of a specific workflow with proprietary data + net retention >120% as it nears SPC stage.

tailwinds.name: Vertical copilots for regulated professions
tailwinds.why: LLM reasoning crossed the bar for law, notarial work, medical billing and regulated finance — long-stagnant categories with deep workflow lock-in and proprietary-data moats. PitchBook: software without a data moat faces "failure to thrive."
tailwinds.watch: Depth of workflow integration + proprietary data that improves with scale — the differentiation budget can’t buy.

market.t: Physical AI is the consensus "next wave."
market.d: Seedcamp raised its biggest-ever $320M fund centered on AI touching the physical world; investors see Europe’s deep-tech edge here.

market.t: Agent-speed workloads break the enterprise backend.
market.d: a16z Big Ideas 2026: recursive, bursty agent fan-out forces a re-architected control plane — "agent-native" infra and AgentOps.

market.t: The moat moved to AgentOps + data moats.
market.d: PitchBook: frontier LLMs are commoditized rentable engines; value is in the orchestration layer. ~20% of software is "failure to thrive" without a data moat.

market.t: AI-native services sell completed work, not seats.
market.d: Sequoia’s 2026 thesis hardens around long-horizon agents and outcome-based "digital labor"; ~40% of enterprise software to embed task-specific agents.

market.t: Financial & enterprise AI is the resilient applied vertical.
market.d: PitchBook: the only applied vertical growing in both deal count and value — automation, risk, compliance; strong early-stage skew.

market.t: North American funding hit records in H1 2026, AI-driven.
market.d: Crunchbase: AI, energy and biotech led the biggest rounds; seed/angle dollars actually dipped in Q2 (~$4.9B) — selectivity rising.

engineers.d: Agent that browses, fills forms, runs shell/code, writes its own skills. The breakout of 2026.
Turns any website into clean, AI-ready data — context API for agents.
Makes your AI agent "think like the laziest senior dev" — fast-rising agent tool.
Drag-and-drop agent pipeline builders — domain experts, not just ML engineers.
Run LLMs entirely on your own hardware — the on-device / edge signal.
Supply-chain scanning — agentic tooling for physical/industrial ops.
Reliability for AI via large-scale simulation & evaluation (also a funded raise).
Share the files your AI agents create with teammates.
